items.title: AP: New Medicaid work requirement sets 80-hours-per-month standard for many expansion adults
items.summary: The Associated Press reports that adults ages 19–64 in Medicaid expansion coverage will have to show they work or do community service at least 80 hours per month, or attend school at least half time. The story says some people can self-attest in 2027 (and once in 2028), but at renewal in 2028 they’ll need to provide proof to keep the exemption if claiming medical frailty.

items.title: KFF: CMS proposal would cap certain Medicaid supplemental payments at 100%/110% of Medicare rates
items.summary: KFF says a CMS proposed rule released in May 2026 would implement new limits on Medicaid state directed payments (SDPs), with caps generally at 100% of Medicare rates in ACA expansion states and 110% in non-expansion states. KFF notes CMS estimates the changes would reduce federal Medicaid spending by $510 billion between 2026 and 2035.

items.title: USDA opens FY 2026 SNAP technology grants (PTIG) with up to $5M and 6–12 expected awards
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service says the SNAP Process and Technology Improvement Grants (PTIG) program will provide up to $5 million and expects to make between six and 12 awards in FY 2026. Eligible applicants include SNAP-administering state agencies and certain public or private nonprofit entities, but USDA says any lead entity that received a PTIG award in FY 2024 or FY 2025 is not eligible to apply in FY 2026.

items.title: USDA publishes Privacy Act system notice for USDA/FNS-15 National SNAP Information Database
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service published a Privacy Act system-of-records notice for USDA/FNS-15, the National SNAP Information Database, describing how participant and transaction data may be used for eligibility validation and program integrity. The notice says the system will be hosted in a FedRAMP-certified FNS AWS cloud environment with data processed and stored in the continental U.S., and it lists routine uses including disclosures to agencies for audit/oversight and to Treasury’s Do Not Pay system.

items.title: SSA posts 2026 schedule: Social Security paid by birth-date Wednesdays; SSI typically paid on the 1st
items.summary: SSA’s 2026–2027 payment schedule says Social Security benefits are paid on the 2nd Wednesday for birthdays on the 1st–10th, the 3rd Wednesday for birthdays on the 11th–20th, and the 4th Wednesday for birthdays on the 21st–31st. SSA also notes that if you received Social Security before May 1997 or if you receive both Social Security and SSI, then Social Security is paid on the 3rd of the month and SSI on the 1st.

items.title: IRS reminder: extension filers still have free options, including IRS Free File for AGI $89,000 or less
items.summary: The IRS says people who requested an extension for their 2025 federal return have until Oct. 15 to file, and it highlights free filing options that are still available. IRS Free File is available to taxpayers with adjusted gross income of $89,000 or less in 2025, and the IRS also points filers to Free File Fillable Forms and MilTax (which includes up to five free state returns for eligible military users).

items.title: IRS disaster tax relief: San Carlos Apache Tribe deadlines postponed to Sept. 28, 2026
items.summary: The IRS announced tax relief for individuals and businesses in the San Carlos Apache Tribe affected by severe storms and flooding (Oct. 10–13, 2025), postponing many filing and payment deadlines to Sept. 28, 2026. The relief applies to returns and payments with original or extended due dates on or after Oct. 10, 2025 and before Sept. 28, 2026, including certain quarterly payroll and excise tax returns that would normally be due Oct. 31, 2025; Jan. 31, 2026; April 30, 2026; and July 31, 2026.

items.title: Education Department: Repayment Assistance Plan (RAP) and Tiered Standard repayment available July 1, 2026
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education says borrowers can access two new repayment options starting July 1, 2026: the Repayment Assistance Plan (RAP) and a Tiered Standard plan with fixed terms of 10, 15, 20, or 25 years. ED also says certain borrowers in repayment plans being phased out (with loans made before July 1, 2026) will have until July 1, 2028 to choose RAP, Tiered Standard, or Income-Based Repayment.
